extension_name: Devinization Pack 2 — Runtime Routing & Work Control
purpose: Define the runtime-control layer that lets the Station Chief turn one user command into an organized operation: intake, route, decompose, assign, checkpoint, resume, monitor, and complete work without waking the entire agency as expensive live execution.
core_operating_philosophy: The Agent Command Center remains a massive command civilization with selective live deployment. The whole 175-family agency remains available for awareness, routing, governance, memory, and context. This pack defines how work moves through the agency without becoming chaos soup.
activation_rule: Use this pack when the user says or implies: route the task, runtime routing, workflow control, work queue, task intake, scope this, decompose this, build work packets, route through the agency, choose families, choose departments, checkpoint this, resume work, long-running task, continue where we left off, don’t lose progress, assign the agents, orchestrate the swarm, create operation brief, Station Chief runtime, one command to the agency.

runtime_activation_tiers.name: Passive Whole-Org Awareness
runtime_activation_tiers.description: The full 175-family agency map remains available as structure, context, memory, governance, and routing intelligence.

runtime_activation_tiers.name: Council Scan
runtime_activation_tiers.description: The Station Chief and routing layer identify which families may be relevant without launching expensive execution.

runtime_activation_tiers.name: Command Brief
runtime_activation_tiers.description: Relevant command layers produce a scoped operation brief with objective, allowed actions, forbidden actions, required outputs, and success criteria.

runtime_activation_tiers.name: Active Operation
runtime_activation_tiers.description: Selected execution crews or workers are activated for actual work, with budget and scope controls.

runtime_activation_tiers.name: Audit, Archive & Continuity Lock
runtime_activation_tiers.description: Scribes, auditors, state memory, checkpointing, and archive logic preserve results and make the operation resumable.

global_rules: 1. Preserve the locked 175-family baseline unless the user explicitly authorizes baseline mutation.
2. Use overlays for Devinization unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
3. Never wake the entire agency as live execution by default.
4. Keep the whole agency available for passive awareness, routing, governance, memory, and context.
6. Create operation briefs before broad execution.
7. Convert tasks into explicit work packets before assigning agents.
8. Every work packet must include objective, scope, allowed files/actions, forbidden files/actions, required outputs, and success criteria.
10. Every execution run must create an output artifact or a blocked-reason artifact.
11. Resume from prior checkpoints instead of restarting from scratch.
13. Do not ask repeated questions when context already contains the answer.
14. Use deterministic demo mode before live/expensive execution when possible.
15. Treat “check please” as verification, not continuation.
16. Treat “ok” or “next” as continuation only when an active task pointer exists.
17. Preserve the art piece: all employees exist, all families can be considered, only live execution is budgeted.

crews.mission: Maintain the active work queue, paused tasks, blocked tasks, next tasks, and review-ready tasks for the Station Chief runtime.
Track whether each operation is intake, scoped, routed, assigned, executing, blocked, verifying, complete, or archived.
Identify blockers, classify whether they are missing info, tool failure, validator failure, scope conflict, permission issue, or credential issue, and recommend a path forward.
Decide when work must pause for human review, validator review, auditor review, or final lock review.
Ensure every operation has a clear brief before execution: objective, context, scope, constraints, outputs, validators, and stop conditions.
Preserve continuity across “ok,” “next,” “check please,” interruptions, thread moves, and long gaps without losing task order.
Decide which command layers, family chiefs, department chiefs, or worker crews should become active for a task while preserving whole-agency awareness.
Coordinate family-level chiefs so relevant families provide routing judgment without turning every family into live execution.
Coordinate department-level managers, scribes, and auditors for selected families and convert command guidance into assignable work.
Activate only the worker roles needed for execution, with defined task packets, budgets, and reporting requirements.
Decide which work can run in parallel, which work must be sequential, and which outputs depend on prior steps.
Prevent uncontrolled agent sprawl, duplicated work, runaway execution, redundant outputs, and pointless model calls.
Maintain the map that translates task types into likely families, departments, overlays, crews, and validators.
Define handoff packets between families/departments so context, outputs, and constraints do not get lost.
Represent dependencies between families, departments, crews, outputs, validators, files, tools, and approvals.
Route unresolved conflicts, blockers, validator failures, tool failures, or scope disputes to the right command/governance layer.
Resolve cases where multiple families could own the work or where ownership is ambiguous.
Identify dependency risks before execution, including missing files, missing tools, unknown context, unsafe writes, unavailable credentials, or validator gaps.
Convert raw user input into a structured intake record with task type, project context, urgency, risk level, and likely next action.
Define exactly what is in scope, out of scope, allowed, forbidden, required, and optional for a task.
Detect missing requirements and decide whether to resolve from context, proceed with assumptions, or ask one necessary question.
Classify work as question, verification, prompt-writing, build, patch, audit, research, file operation, memory capture, planning, or correction.
Define the testable conditions that make a task complete, including required artifacts, validators, scope check, and final report.
Package intake results into a handoff-ready operation packet for decomposition, routing, or execution.
Convert scope into precise task boundaries so the system does not mix planning, coding, validating, committing, and reporting unless requested.
Break the task into major steps, subtasks, deliverables, validators, dependencies, and stop points.
Create assignable work packages with owner, objective, scope, inputs, outputs, budget, validators, and reporting rules.
Assign ownership to family chiefs, department chiefs, overlay crews, workers, scribes, or auditors based on task type and routing matrix.
Decide the order of work, what must happen first, what can happen in parallel, and when verification should occur.
Detect missing steps, overlapping steps, vague outputs, impossible task packets, and hidden scope creep before execution.
Decide where checkpoints should occur before, during, and after a task so work can resume without losing context.
Capture current state, completed work, pending work, blockers, changed files, validators run, and next action.
Define how tasks restart after interruption, tool failure, model limit, thread move, or user pause.
Compare current work against original scope and stop the run if unexpected files, actions, or goals appear.
Handle user interruptions, task pivots, tool errors, context loss, and message cutoffs while preserving active state.
Preserve partial outputs as artifacts instead of letting unfinished work disappear or become “no output.”
Create compact bridge summaries that let future runs continue from prior work without rereading the entire project.
Estimate remaining work, identify next milestones, flag likely blockers, and recommend whether to split the task.
Enforce long-task rules: checkpoints, progress reports, output artifacts, stop conditions, validators, and no fake completion.
Record run history, commands, decisions, outputs, errors, validators, and continuation notes.
Generate exact next commands or prompts needed to resume a task from the current checkpoint.
Summarize resumability health across active tasks, including checkpoint status, blocked items, partial outputs, and next actions.

ownership_metadata.ownership_phrase: Project owner, system architect, and operating-doctrine author: Devin O’Rourke.
ownership_metadata.applies_to: Agent Command Center Devinization overlay architecture
ownership_metadata.baseline_note: The locked 175-family baseline remains preserved; this metadata identifies Devin O’Rourke as owner/architect of the Devinization overlay stack.
